title: Virus Concentration and Infection
author: Aditya Mohan
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8h9ht96

steps.title: Harvest the lentivirus-containing supernatants. (Caution
steps.instruction: Harvest the lentivirus-containing supernatants. (Caution: supernatants contain live lentivirus.) Pool similar stocks, if desired. Filter through a 0.45 μm filter.

steps.title: Prepare RetroNectin solution (30 ug/mL) by diluting RetroNectin powder (0
steps.instruction: Prepare RetroNectin solution (30 ug/mL) by diluting RetroNectin powder (0.5 mg) into 16.6 mL of PBS

steps.title: Add the retrovirus stock solution or...
steps.instruction: Add the retrovirus stock solution or diluted solution at 125 - 500 μl/cm2 to the RetroNectin-coated plate. (Approx 1.5 mL)

steps.title: Transfer clarified supernatant to a sterile...
steps.instruction: Transfer clarified supernatant to a sterile container and combine 1 volume of Lenti-X Concentrator with 3 volumes of clarified supernatant. Mix by gentle inversion. Larger volumes may be accommodated through the use of larger (i.e., 250 ml or 500 ml) centrifuge tubes.

steps.title: Incubate mixture at 4°C for 30 minutes to overnight
steps.instruction: Incubate mixture at 4°C for 30 minutes to overnight. 4 °C 00:30:00

steps.title: Centrifuge sample at 1,500 x g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge sample at 1,500 x g for 45 minutes at 4°C. After centrifugation, an off-white pellet will be visible. 1500 x g 4 °C

steps.title: Dispense an appropriate volume of sterile...
steps.instruction: Dispense an appropriate volume of sterile RetroNectin solution into each well (1.5 mL) per 6 well dish.

steps.title: Carefully remove supernatant, taking care not to disturb the pellet
steps.instruction: Carefully remove supernatant, taking care not to disturb the pellet. Residual supernatant can be removed with either a pipette tip or by brief centrifugation at 1,500 x g.

steps.title: Gently resuspend the pellet in 1/10...
steps.instruction: Gently resuspend the pellet in 1/10 to 1/100th of the original volume using complete DMEM, PBS, or TNE. The pellet can be somewhat sticky at first but will go into suspension quickly.

steps.title: Keep at room temperature for 30 minutes
steps.instruction: Keep at room temperature for 30 minutes. Room temperature 02:00:00

steps.title: Remove the RetroNectin solution and then...
steps.instruction: Remove the RetroNectin solution and then block with an appropriate volume of sterile 2% bovine serum albumin (BSA, Fraction V) in PBS (1.5 mL of a 6 well dish) Allow the plate to stand at room temperature for 30 minutes. Room temperature 00:30:00

steps.title: Remove the BSA solution, and wash...
steps.instruction: Remove the BSA solution, and wash the plate once with an appropriate volume of HBSS/Hepes or PBS. After removing the wash solution, the plate is ready for use.

steps.title: Place the plate in a centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Place the plate in a centrifuge pre-warmed to 32℃ and centrifuge for 2 hours at 32℃ at 1,000 - 2,000g to facilitate binding of virus particles with RetroNectin reagent. 02:00:00 32 °C 2000 x g

steps.title: Discard the supernatant, but do not...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant, but do not allow the plate to dry. Wash the plate with an appropriate volume of PBS or PBS containing 0.1 - 2% albumin (BSA or HSA).

steps.title: Collect the target cells and count...
steps.instruction: Collect the target cells and count the number of living cells. Then suspend the cells in the growth medium at a concentration of 0.2 - 1 x 105 cells/ml.

steps.title: Do not allow the plate to dry
steps.instruction: Do not allow the plate to dry. Immediately add target cells at a density of 0.5 - 2.5 x 104 cells/cm2. * 6 well dish has SA of 9.6 cm2

steps.title: To promote contact between the target...
steps.instruction: To promote contact between the target cells and viral particles, plates can be centrifuged after adding the cells. 2000 g for 20 minutes 2000 x g 00:20:00

steps.title: Incubate in a 37℃, 5% CO2...
steps.instruction: Incubate in a 37℃, 5% CO2 incubator for 2 - 3 days.
